name: SQL Injection (Error-Based Detection)
description: Tests for error-based SQL injection by injecting common payloads into URL parameters and checking for database error messages in the response. Covers MySQL, PostgreSQL, MSSQL, Oracle, and SQLite error patterns.
author: AC3 ScanForge

values: You have an error in your SQL syntax
Microsoft OLE DB Provider for SQL Server

remediation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements for all database interactions. Implement input validation and output encoding. Use an ORM where possible. Apply least-privilege database accounts.

intelligence.dfirRelevance: SQL injection remains the most exploited web vulnerability. Attackers use SQLi to exfiltrate databases, escalate privileges, and establish persistence through stored procedures or file writes.
